title: AST Officer Involved Shooting Investigation: Bethel [AK26026271]
summary: At the request of the Bethel Police Department, the Alaska Bureau of Investigation is investigating an officer-involved shooting that occurred in Bethel on March 25, 2026, at approximately 4:05 am. A preliminary investigation indicates an adult male had been taken to the Bethel hospital earlier in the night after standing in the roadway and refusing to move onto the sidewalk. The man left the hospital before being seen and later returned to the roadway. Officers contacted the man a second time, at which point he produced a knife and refused multiple commands to drop it. The man then approached an officer while brandishing the knife, and the officer fired, striking him. The man was medevaced to an Anchorage-area hospital for treatment of serious injuries. No other injuries were reported to
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST VCoR: Fairbanks [AK26026208]
summary: On Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at approximately 10:27PM AST responded to a residence in reference to a domestic disturbance. On scene, it was determined nothing criminal or domestic related happened, however the subject had violated conditions by consuming alcohol. The subject was no longer on scene, but was found three doors down in a neighbor's house and subsequently arrested for violating conditions of release. The subject was transported to Fairbanks Correctional Center for further detention.

title: AST Criminal Trespass II: Fairbanks [AK26025505]
summary: On March 22, 2026, at approximately 10:48 p.m., the Alaska State Troopers responded to a residence on Old Elliott Highway for a report of a disturbance. Troopers contacted 38-year-old Fairbanks resident Benjamin John Clement Mulberger at the back door of the home after he had been repeatedly told by the resident not to return to the property. The investigation determined Mulberger had unlawfully returned and attempted to contact the resident. Mulberger was arrested for Criminal Trespass in the Second Degree and transported to Fairbanks Correctional Center.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

charges: Criminal Trespass in the Second Degree

summary: In 1920, over a hundred years ago, Congress passed the Merchant Marine Act. title: Minister’s Message: Bright and vivid reasons
summary: Sunrises and sunsets are beautiful in the winter months. I say winter because I don’t usually see them during the summer; they are too early and too late. I don’t often see the sun begin and end its journey across the sky in the summer unless I’m going fishing or coming home from a fishing trip.
